position_summary: Claude's position has a real, defensible differentiator in the repo: CS-native hypothesis generation plus deterministic testing/falsification is documented, and the preserve-intent claim should not be collapsed into “they used Claude Science.” The weak points are operational and calibration-heavy: the final video harness is not as finished as the script status implies, and several spoken lines compress caveats enough that a hostile judge could read them as overclaiming novelty, liveness, or causal closure.

findings.title: The CS capture plan is still Friday-critical, despite being described as de-risked
findings.evidence: `docs/demo-video-pack/cs/scenes.mjs` still has TODOs for the actual money shots: open `receipt.md` for b5 and reach `review.json`/calibrated-language finding for b6b. `docs/demo-video-pack/cs/CAPTURE_PLAN.md` says b5 is only in an artifact, b6b is up-transcript or `review.json`, window wheel does not scroll the transcript, artifact thumbnail selectors are not known, and bottom b6b only shows a weaker cosmetic finding. The script status nevertheless says capture path de-risked and screen-only gate PASS.
findings.concern_path: On Friday, the run can technically produce a video while missing the funnel/NAB2 receipt and reviewer-overclaim receipt. That would leave the strongest claims narrated over generic CS UI or weaker evidence, hurting Demo 30% and Claude Use 25%.
findings.suggested_action: Treat b5 artifact-open and b6b reviewer-proof as release blockers, not tuning. Pre-record or screenshot those exact receipts first; only then assemble the final screen-only sequence.

findings.title: The live-vs-cached boundary is still too easy to misread in the narration
findings.evidence: `docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md` states the full 3,935-gene Stage 1 sweep replayed cached Europe PMC/Open Targets JSON receipts with zero live calls, while only Stage 0/micro-sweep and Stage 3 S3 were live. The video script places “CS builds LBD live” immediately before “22,000 machine-generated hypotheses go in,” and the written summary says every machine-generated hypothesis is culled inside Claude Science without foregrounding cached replay.
findings.concern_path: A judge could conclude the 22,039-hypothesis funnel was freshly crawled live in CS. If later they read the README, the submission looks slippery even though the underlying work is honest.
findings.suggested_action: Make the spoken/video distinction explicit: live micro-sweep proves CS can author/run the generator; the full funnel is a CS-native cached-receipt replay; STAT6 falsification is live S3. Do not rely on guardrails hidden later in the doc.

findings.title: “The literature had never drawn” is over-absolute unless carefully scoped
findings.evidence: `docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md` calls NAB2→eczema “near-novel” with 6 prior literature co-mentions and later says an audit found zero papers directly linking NAB2 to Th1/Th2 polarization or atopic eczema. The script and `swanson-graphic.html` use the shorter “a connection the literature had never drawn.”
findings.concern_path: The phrase is defensible only if “drawn” means direct mechanistic NAB2→Th1/Th2→atopic-eczema chain, not any NAB2/eczema co-mention. A reviewer can attack this as novelty inflation because `ac_lit=6` is part of the receipt.
findings.suggested_action: Use “no direct chain we found” or “six co-mentions, but no direct NAB2→Th1/Th2→eczema chain” where space allows. This preserves the novel claim while making the receipt self-consistent.

findings.title: The STAT6 confounder line closes more than the evidence closes
findings.evidence: The NAB2 finding doc says CRISPRi cis artifact is refuted because STAT6 is unmoved under NAB2 knockdown, but it also says the disease label is Open Targets genetic/GWAS evidence and could in principle be LD-inherited from the STAT6 12q13 atopy locus; NAB2 and STAT6 share the same disease profile. The script says “Its hardest confounder — refuted live” without naming the narrower cis-artifact scope.
findings.concern_path: The video may imply all STAT6-shadow concerns are closed. The repo itself only closes the perturbation cis-repression artifact, while the GWAS/LD disease-label caveat remains.
findings.suggested_action: Say “the CRISPRi cis-artifact concern was refuted live” rather than “hardest confounder” alone, or add “the GWAS label remains a nomination.”

findings.title: The plan under-sells the repo’s strongest falsification thesis
findings.evidence: `CLAUDE.md` says the thesis is the confident receipt-backed NO and that falsification, not confirmation, is the moat. The 3-minute script spends roughly the first 1:55 on floods/LBD/platform setup and gives the NO only one phrase, without a concrete failed-knockdown or refuted example named on screen.
findings.concern_path: The novel generation+testing platform claim is protected, but the actual “referee” moat may land as a funnel statistic rather than a memorable capability. That weakens Depth/Execution and Claude Use because the judge may remember the NAB2 yes, not the artifact-catching no.
findings.suggested_action: Put one concrete NO receipt on screen and in words, even briefly: failed knockdown → untested, off-target flag, or SBF2 effect-refuted. Make refusal evidence visible, not just asserted.

findings.title: The CS-native cut may weaken the Demo score if it abandons the already working Streamlit MVP too completely
findings.evidence: The script says the Streamlit app is skipped and the app remains only supporting. Deadline context says the Streamlit app plus prior demo already exist as fallback MVP. The CS cut depends on local auth, hard-coded frame URLs, receipt-artifact selectors, transcription gate, ElevenLabs, and music mux.
findings.concern_path: A pure CS screen recording can score high on Claude Use, but if the UI capture is brittle or visually opaque, Demo 30% suffers. The existing app is a fallback for showing a polished, judge-readable artifact.
findings.suggested_action: Keep CS-native as the main submission only if the receipt shots are crisp. Otherwise use a hybrid fallback: CS-native proof of method plus the app for the human-readable referee result.

findings.title: The title/endcap introduces a stronger product claim than the evidence needs
findings.evidence: `demo.config.mjs` endcap says “Generate a hypothesis. Test it. One platform.” and the narration says “Now there’s one platform that can generate a hypothesis and validate it in the same place.” The documented full generation was a cached-receipt replay, the live generator was a 12-gene micro-sweep, and cross-model independence remains external by design.
findings.concern_path: The line is rhetorically strong but can sound like a generally available integrated platform rather than a hackathon-built instrument demonstrated on a narrow case. It risks a product-adoption vibe, contrary to the Researcher-track framing.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the differentiator but ground it: “In this workbench, I built the missing generation half beside the test.” That keeps the one-platform novelty without implying product maturity.

findings.title: The “Reviewer killed my overclaim” beat depends on a hidden artifact and may be visually weak
findings.evidence: `docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md` has strong evidence that Sonnet-5 flagged “validated” and “definitive.” But `CAPTURE_PLAN.md` says b6b’s bottom view only shows a weaker cosmetic finding and the calibrated-language finding requires scroll-up or opening `review.json`. `scenes.mjs` has only a TODO for this.
findings.concern_path: If the capture cannot show the exact reviewer finding, the narration will over-index on an invisible claim. The self-check beat is valuable, but only if the viewer can see the receipt.
findings.suggested_action: Use a prepared artifact view or static overlay sourced from `review.json` if live scrolling is unreliable. The video must visibly show “validated/definitive” being flagged.

findings.title: The Swanson/fish-oil setup consumes time that may not pay off enough for judges
findings.evidence: The beat sheet gives nearly 30 seconds to Swanson’s historical fish-oil/Raynaud’s example and another 25 seconds to personal core-facility framing before any Claude Science or PyZoBot-specific result appears.
findings.concern_path: For a 3-minute judged demo, the setup is elegant but expensive. It delays Claude Use, working system proof, and biological impact until the back half.
findings.suggested_action: Compress historical Swanson to the minimum needed for ABC, and spend the recovered seconds on the live micro-sweep receipt, the NO receipt, and the NAB2/STAT6 evidence.

findings.title: Some repo-facing status text is stale or internally inconsistent
findings.evidence: The script beat table still says “Framing slide — feature matrix (TODO build)” while the status says framing slides are built and the asset file exists. The script says capture path de-risked and screen-only gate PASS, while `scenes.mjs` and `README.md` still require Friday tuning. `demo.config.mjs` uses `ELLEVENLABS_API_KEY`, which looks misspelled relative to the common `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` convention, though it may match the user’s env.
findings.concern_path: These are not scientific failures, but they are exactly the kind of small inconsistencies that burn the final day or make a reviewer distrust the checklist.
findings.suggested_action: Clean the status language to separate built assets, syntax/gate checks, and final receipt-capture blockers. Verify the ElevenLabs env var spelling before the Friday run.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Force Claude to answer the two release-blocker questions: exactly how will the final video visibly prove b5 funnel/NAB2 and b6b reviewer-overclaim, and exactly how will the narration distinguish live micro-sweep, cached full sweep, and live S3 falsification without weakening the one-platform novelty claim?
